files_changed.summary: New command definition for /neuralrelay history — reads handoff chains, extracts summary data from requirements and review-final handoffs, displays last 5 runs in a formatted table
Added history command entry to the commands array referencing commands/history.md

unit_tests_written.summary: No unit tests written — this is a markdown command definition, not executable code. Testing requires running the command against a real handoff directory with various states.

coverage_gaps: No test for corrupted or malformed JSON in handoff files — if 04-review-agent.json contains invalid JSON, the command's Bash-based JSON parsing will fail with an unclear error instead of showing 'incomplete' status

Behavior with a very large number of chain directories (>100) is untested — the Glob tool returns all matches and the command sorts them all to find the last 5, which could be slow on large handoff histories

No test for chain directories that exist but are completely empty (no handoff files at all) — this could happen if the process manager creates the directory but the first agent fails before writing anything
The comma-formatting logic for token numbers has not been tested with values exceeding 999,999 — the Bash printf approach may behave differently across shell implementations

properties_believed.property: Output is sorted newest-first by chain ID because chain IDs use YYYYMMDD-HHmmss format and reverse-alphabetical sort equals reverse-chronological sort
properties_believed.evidence: Chain ID format is defined in commands/start.md and the history command uses reverse sort on directory names — YYYYMMDD-HHmmss is lexicographically sortable

properties_believed.property: The command correctly displays exactly min(5, total_chains) entries — no off-by-one errors in the slice logic
properties_believed.evidence: Used head -5 on the sorted directory list in the Bash command, but this was only mentally traced, not tested with exactly 5 directories

properties_believed.property: Incomplete chains (missing 04-review-agent.json) are displayed with 'incomplete' status rather than causing the command to fail
properties_believed.evidence: Added a file-existence check before reading 04-review-agent.json, but the fallback path for missing task_summary (when 01-ba-agent.json is also missing) was not tested

properties_believed.property: The command works without neuralrelay.yaml present — it only reads from the .neuralrelay/handoffs/ directory
properties_believed.evidence: The command definition does not reference neuralrelay.yaml anywhere; it only uses Glob on .neuralrelay/handoffs/ and Read on handoff JSON files

known_risks: Shell compatibility: the command instructions use Bash-specific features (printf with comma formatting, associative-like patterns) that may not work identically on all platforms where Claude Code runs
The 5-run limit is hardcoded in the command definition — if we later want to make it configurable, the command would need to accept an optional argument
Race condition if a pipeline is actively running while /neuralrelay history is called — a partially-written handoff file could cause JSON parse errors
integration_points: JSON files: schemas/requirements.schema.json and schemas/review-final.schema.json define the data contracts
.claude-plugin/plugin.json: must be updated to register the new command
